skill.name: gpt-image-2
skill.description: Generate and edit images with GPT Image 2 through the local Codex CLI. The skill uses an existing ChatGPT Plus or Pro subscription and extracts the generated image from new Codex session rollouts.
skill.summary: Generate GPT Image 2 images through Codex CLI using an existing ChatGPT subscription.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, home-directory file access, network references, and base64 decoding. Review found these patterns are mostly expected for a Codex CLI image-generation wrapper, but the skill does run local commands and reads new Codex session rollout files under ~/.codex/sessions. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious exfiltration behavior was found in the reviewed files.

medium_findings.title: Local Codex CLI Execution and Session Rollout Access
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for external command and filesystem access. The script invokes codex exec and then reads new rollout JSONL files under ~/.codex/sessions to extract the generated image. This is consistent with the skill purpose, but it gives the skill access to local Codex session artifacts created during the run and depends on local CLI behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly invokes codex and intentionally reads session rollout files. The surrounding comments and documentation align this behavior with image extraction rather than covert collection.

medium_findings.title: User Content Leaves the Local Machine Through Codex
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for network-dependent behavior. The skill itself does not add a custom network endpoint, but prompts and reference images are passed to the Codex CLI, which the documentation states contacts OpenAI using the user login.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network transfer is explicit in the documentation and required by the image generation workflow. I found no evidence of extra telemetry or third-party callbacks.

low_findings.title: Base64 Decode Used for Image Extraction
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for obfuscation. scripts/extract_image.py searches JSONL session content for large base64 strings with PNG, JPEG, or WebP magic prefixes and decodes the selected image payload to the requested output file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The decode path is limited to image magic prefixes and writes bytes to an image file. It is not used to hide or execute code.

low_findings.title: Temporary Logs and Output Path Writes Are Bounded
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for broad filesystem abuse. The shell script creates temporary log files and removes them with a trap, while the Python helper restricts output to image extensions and rejects system directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant filesystem writes are visible and constrained. Residual risk remains because the user controls the output path within the allowed image path policy.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Are Non-Secret References
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for suspicious network indicators. The hardcoded URLs are homepage, repository, documentation, and example image links in SKILL.md, not executable callbacks or credential destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in Markdown content and prerequisite instructions. I found no code path that posts secrets or generated data to these URLs.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill calls codex and python3 from a shell script. Arguments are array-quoted, but users should understand that local commands run during generation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command invocations are direct and central to the skill. The implementation avoids obvious shell interpolation for the prompt.

dangerous_patterns.title: Home Directory Session File Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The script reads new Codex session rollout files under ~/.codex/sessions after a generation run. This is documented, but it is sensitive local state.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The home-directory path is explicit and the stated purpose is image payload extraction. The pre/post diff narrows scope to new rollout files.

content.user_title: Generate GPT Image 2 Visuals in Claude Code
content.value_statement: Teams need image generation inside their coding workflow without adding a separate image API account. This skill routes GPT Image 2 requests through the local Codex CLI and returns saved image files.
actual_capabilities: Generate new images from text prompts using GPT Image 2 through Codex CLI.
Edit or restyle reference images passed with repeatable reference flags.
Save generated PNG, JPEG, or WebP image files to a caller-selected path.
Extract image payloads from new Codex session rollout files after generation.
Report clear exit codes for missing tools, bad paths, failed Codex runs, and missing image payloads.
limitations: Requires a local Codex CLI installation and an active Codex login.
Requires a ChatGPT plan with GPT Image 2 image generation entitlement.
Depends on Codex session rollout persistence and may break if CLI internals change.
Does not provide direct OpenAI API access or separate billing support.

use_cases.title: Product Mockup Drafts
use_cases.description: Create quick visual concepts for product pages, ads, or launch materials directly from a project workspace.

use_cases.title: Reference-Based Restyling
use_cases.description: Transform existing images into new visual styles while keeping the original subject or composition as a guide.

use_cases.title: Developer Asset Generation
use_cases.description: Produce placeholder illustrations, icons, and interface imagery without leaving Claude Code or Codex workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a single generated image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GPT Image 2 to generate a clean product illustration of [subject]. Save it as [output path].

prompt_templates.title: Style Transfer
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt using one reference image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GPT Image 2 with [reference image path]. Restyle it as [style], preserving the main composition. Save the result as [output path].

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Reference Composition
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt combining multiple visual references.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GPT Image 2 with these references: [path one], [path two], and [path three]. Combine their key visual traits into one coherent image saved as [output path].

prompt_templates.title: Production Asset Pass
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt with layout and quality requirements.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use GPT Image 2 to create a polished [asset type] for [audience]. Follow these constraints: [dimensions], [brand traits], [text requirements], and [output path].

output_examples.input: Generate a square poster for a developer meetup with bold typography and a bright modern style.
output_examples.output: A saved poster image file is produced, and the assistant returns the output path for review.

output_examples.input: Use a reference logo image and create a clean T-shirt mockup with the logo centered.
output_examples.output: A new mockup image is saved with the requested visual treatment and the reference image used as input.

output_examples.input: Restyle a flat icon as a traditional woodblock print while keeping the same subject.
output_examples.output: The result is an image file with the original subject preserved and the requested style applied.

best_practices: Use explicit output paths with image extensions such as PNG, JPG, JPEG, or WebP.
Keep reference images in accessible local paths before invoking the skill.
Tell users that prompts and reference images are processed through their Codex login.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill when the user did not request the GPT Image 2 route.
Do not treat it as a direct OpenAI API client or standalone image service.
Do not use it for confidential images unless the user accepts Codex and OpenAI processing.

faq.question: Does this skill include GPT Image 2 access?
faq.answer: No. It requires the user to already have Codex CLI access and a ChatGPT plan with image generation enabled.

faq.question: Can it edit existing images?
faq.answer: Yes. The script supports one or more reference images for image editing, restyling, or composition.

faq.question: Where are generated images saved?
faq.answer: Images are saved to the output path supplied by the caller, using PNG, JPG, JPEG, or WebP extensions.

faq.question: Does the skill make its own network calls?
faq.answer: No custom endpoint is implemented. Codex CLI still contacts OpenAI as part of the requested generation workflow.

faq.question: Why does it read Codex session files?
faq.answer: Codex stores generated image payloads in rollout files, so the script reads new rollout files created by the current run.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it executes local commands and reads Codex session rollout files.
